runs.reasoningSummary: From memory, empirical estimates cluster around 0.4-1.0 with many normalized at 1 (Cobb-Douglas), but post-2000 studies using normalized CES favor values below 1 (~0.6-0.8) for long-run aggregate U.S. data; distribution reflects range from low-substitution to slight super-unitary outliers.
